column2: Black PVD Titanium G10 Military Watch with 300m Water Resistance, Sapphire Crystal, 10 Year Battery Life and GTLS Tritium Illumination
column3:
SKU Code: T/12/SL23PV

MWC Combat Elite 300m Black PVD Titanium G10 – Swiss Ronda 715li Movement with Tritium GTLS Illumination

This is the latest self-luminous G10 model from MWC’s Combat Elite range. Built for extreme durability, it features a titanium case, 300m (1000ft) water resistance, a screw-down crown and caseback, and a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal. For maximum night-time visibility, the hands are fitted with Tritium GTLS light sources that provide continuous illumination without the need for external charging.

Covert Black PVD Finish – Military-Grade Durability

The matte black, non-reflective PVD finish used on this watch is frequently specified in military contracts for special forces units and counterterrorism teams. Unlike cheaper painted finishes that fade or wear off, PVD plating alters the titanium at a molecular level, ensuring exceptional durability even in extreme conditions. However, while PVD coatings withstand heavy use, they can be compromised by severe physical damage, such as deep gouges or excessive abrasion against rough surfaces.

Unparalleled Performance & Cutting-Edge Technology

The MWC 300m Black PVD Titanium G10 with a Swiss Ronda 715li movement is among the highest-spec general service watches in the world. It incorporates the best of both traditional and modern watchmaking technology, combining:
✔ A lightweight yet ultra-strong titanium case
✔ A high-precision, ultra-low-maintenance movement
✔ Self-luminous GTLS Tritium vials for unbeatable night visibility
✔ A shatterproof, scratch-resistant sapphire crystal

This combination makes it the ultimate timepiece for military, law enforcement, and security personnel.

Exceptional Night Visibility – Tritium GTLS Technology

This watch is designed for maximum readability in low-light and total darkness. The hour markers and hands each feature self-luminous Tritium vials, which glow continuously without requiring battery power or external charging. This advanced system is:

  • 100 times brighter than traditional luminous paint
  • Maintenance-free for over 10 years
  • Regulated under U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (10 CFR 30.15) for safety

Designed for Military Deployment – Long Battery Life

Originally developed for a government contract, this model was specifically designed for long-term storage and deployment. Many military watches struggle with dead batteries when stored for extended periods, but this issue is completely avoided with the Swiss Ronda 715li movement, which offers an impressive 10-year battery life.

Built for Extreme Conditions – Superior to Standard Steel G10 Models

MWC’s G10 models have been military-proven since the 1980s, and this Combat Elite version takes performance to the next level. While maintaining a design similar to its steel counterparts, the titanium case places it in a class of its own.

The watch comes with a black NATO-style nylon military strap, and the caseback features official contract markings.

Specifications:

✔ Case Diameter: 40mm (excluding crown)
✔ Case Diameter: 42mm (including crown)
✔ Lug to Lug: 46mm
✔ Thickness: 13mm
✔ Weight: 52g
✔ Dial Color: Black
✔ Case Material: PVD Titanium
✔ Caseback: Screw-down
✔ Crown: Screw-down
✔ Water Resistance: 300m / 1000ft
✔ Crystal: Sapphire with anti-reflective coating
✔ Movement: Swiss Ronda 715li Quartz
✔ Battery Life: 10 years
✔ Spring Bar Type: Standard
✔ Strap: Black 20mm NATO-style nylon military strap

Why Sapphire Crystal?

Many customers ask about the benefits of sapphire crystal over standard mineral glass. Here’s why sapphire is the superior choice:

✔ Highly resistant to shattering and scratches – perfect for military, police, and security personnel
✔ Extremely durable under tough conditions
✔ Over 90% of cracked watch crystals involve mineral glass, not sapphire
✔ Treated with a heat process to remove internal stresses, increasing strength
✔ Finished with two layers of anti-reflective coating for optimal clarity

Although sapphire crystal is a premium feature, it is a long-term investment in durability and reliability, making it the preferred choice for professionals operating in extreme environments.

Final Thoughts

This MWC Combat Elite G10 model offers the ultimate blend of cutting-edge technology, military-grade durability, and superior night visibility. Whether for military deployment, law enforcement, or extreme outdoor use, it is built to withstand the toughest conditions.

✔ Lightweight Titanium Case
✔ Long-Lasting Swiss Movement
✔ Self-Luminous Tritium GTLS for Night Visibility
✔ Sapphire Crystal for Maximum Durability

This is the highest-spec titanium G10 watch on the market today—built for those who demand uncompromising performance.

For maximum nighttime visibility, both the hands and hour markers are equipped with Tritium GTLS light sources, ensuring constant readability in all conditions.

Unrivaled Performance & Cutting-Edge Technology

The MWC 300m Titanium G10, powered by a Swiss Ronda 715li movement, is one of the highest-specification general service watches available worldwide. This model seamlessly blends traditional military watchmaking with modern advancements, making it superior to its competitors.

✔ Titanium Case: Lightweight, durable, and corrosion-resistant

Built to Military Specification

Tritium GTLS Illumination – Superior Night Visibility

✔ 100 times brighter than traditional luminous paint

✔ Built-in safety compliance (U.S. NRC 10 CFR 30.15)

The self-luminous Tritium vials on the hands and markers provide a clear, high-visibility display in low-light or complete darkness. Unlike conventional luminous paint, GTLS technology ensures constant brightness for over 10 years.

Designed for Military Deployment – 10-Year Battery Life

This model was specifically designed for a government contract, with the requirement that watches could be stored for extended periods without worrying about battery depletion. The Swiss Ronda 715li movement was the ideal choice, offering:

✔ A 10-year battery life – eliminating the risk of dead batteries when stored
✔ Precision quartz movement – ensuring long-term reliability
✔ Zero maintenance requirement – perfect for active duty and field deployment

Proven Military Heritage – G10 Series

The MWC G10 series has been one of the most trusted military watch designs since the 1980s. While this model maintains the classic military aesthetic, the titanium case elevates it into an entirely new class.

Why Choose Sapphire Crystal?

Sapphire crystal is the gold standard for durability in professional-grade watches. Here’s why:

✔ Unmatched scratch and shatter resistance
✔ Ideal for military, law enforcement, and security personnel
✔ Over 90% of cracked watch crystals involve mineral glass, not sapphire
✔ Heat-treated for enhanced strength and resilience
✔ Double-layer anti-reflective coating for optimal clarity

While sapphire crystal is typically found in higher-end watches, it is an investment in longevity and reliability, making it the top choice for professionals who demand uncompromising durability.

Final Thoughts

The MWC Combat Elite G10 Titanium is designed for those who demand the best—whether in military operations, law enforcement, or extreme outdoor conditions.

✔ Lightweight Titanium Case – Built for durability and comfort
✔ Swiss Ronda 715li Movement – 10-year battery life
✔ Tritium GTLS Night Visibility – Maintenance-free illumination
✔ Sapphire Crystal – The ultimate in scratch resistance

With unrivaled durability and cutting-edge technology, this is the highest-spec G10 watch available today—a timepiece built for professionals who can’t afford to compromise.

column2: MWC G10 PVD 12/24 200m Water Resistant Military Watch, Sapphire Crystal and 10 Year Battery Life

SKU: G101224PV/200

MWC G10 PVD Military Watch with 12/24 Hour Dial and Sapphire Crystal

A modern evolution of the classic G10 model we first produced in the 1980s, this watch features a U.S.-style 12/24 hour dial format .

Key Features

  • Luminova Dial Markings
    Offers excellent nighttime visibility. Luminova charges via exposure to light and glows through the night without fading permanently—non-radioactive and long-lasting.

  • Matte Black PVD Finish
    The non-reflective, covert finish is specified in military contracts for special forces and covert operations. The PVD process bonds color to the steel at a molecular level, far superior to painted finishes. While resistant to wear, it can show damage from extreme abrasion or gouging.

  • Screw-Down Crown
    Unlike push-in crowns, this locking crown offers enhanced water resistance and durability, especially in demanding environments.

  • Sapphire Crystal
    Highly scratch and shatter-resistant, ideal for military, law enforcement, and outdoor use. Treated with anti-reflective coating for optimal clarity.

Why Sapphire Crystal?

Over 70% of our customers are professionals operating in extreme conditions—military personnel, police officers, and outdoor adventurers. For them, sapphire crystal is essential:

  • Scratch and shatter-resistant
  • Heat-treated to eliminate internal stresses
  • Dual anti-reflective coating
  • Stronger and longer-lasting than mineral glass
  • The same material used in modern smartphones like the iPhone

While more expensive, sapphire is a particularly worthwhile upgrade for anyone needing a truly robust timepiece.

Technical Specifications

  • Case Diameter: 40mm (excluding crown), 42mm (including crown)
  • Case Thickness: 11.5mm
  • Weight: 60g
  • Dial Color: Black
  • Luminosity: Luminova (non-radioactive)
  • Case Material: 316L Stainless Steel with Black PVD Coating
  • Caseback: 316L Stainless Steel with Contract Markings
  • Crown: Screw-Down Locking Crown (316L Steel)
  • Water Resistance: 20 ATM (200m/660ft)
  • Crystal: Sapphire – Scratch & Shatter Resistant with Anti-Reflective Coating
  • Battery: CR2016 with 10 year battery life
  • Movement: Ronda 715li Quartz with Date Window
  • Strap: 20mm NATO Military Webbing Strap (Black)
  • Lugs: Drilled Lugs

If you're after a tough, purpose-built watch with military-grade specs and classic looks, the G101224PV/200 is a proven performer with decades of field experience behind it.

column2: G10SL MKV 200m/660ft Water Resistant Military Watch with GTLS Tritium Light Sources, Sapphire Crystal and 10 Year Battery Life
column3:
SKU Code: G10SL/200

This self-luminous G10 model is a significantly upgraded remake of a popular design from our past. It features a robust stainless steel case with 200m (660ft) water resistance, a screw-down crown, a screw caseback, a scratch-resistant sapphire crystal, and a 10-year battery life.

Combining the best of new and traditional technology, this model retains all the popular features of our conventional G10 Luminova models while incorporating cutting-edge self-luminous GTLS tritium vials. The specification of this watch adheres to American Military Specification Number: US MIL-W-46374F.

Key Features:

Superior Night Visibility: The self-luminous system ensures easy readability at night. The G10SL/200 model features a distinctive face layout, with the hour and minute hands each containing a brightly glowing vial of tritium gas. Additionally, it has two lights at the 12 o'clock position, a unique feature to MWC. The vials contain less than the personal limit of 25 millicuries of radioactive gas, complying with U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission regulations (10 CFR 30.15).

Tritium GTLS Tubes: Military watches of this type are among the easiest to read in low light or total darkness. The self-activated light source is up to 100 times brighter than traditional luminous paint. These light-emitting tubes require no battery power or any form of charging and never need servicing or maintenance. Their life expectancy is at least 10 years, after which they gradually fade.

Durability: The standard MWC G10 is one of our most tried and tested models, with earlier variants in service for over 40 years. This model shares a similar casing to the Luminova models, ensuring the same level of durability as our non-tritium versions.

Heavyweight NATO Webbing Strap: The G10SL/200 is supplied with a robust NATO webbing strap. The reverse of the case features various contract markings.

Specifications:

  • Case Diameter: 40mm (excluding crown), 42mm (including crown)
  • Lug to Lug: 46mm
  • Thickness: 13mm
  • Weight: 66g
  • Dial Color: Black
  • Luminosity: GTLS Tritium Tubes
  • Case Material: 316L Stainless Steel
  • Caseback: Screw-down, 316L Stainless Steel
  • Crown: Screw-down, 316L Stainless Steel
  • Water Resistance: 200m / 660ft
  • Battery: CR2016 Lithium - Estimated life 10 years
  • Crystal: Scratch-resistant and shatterproof sapphire
  • Movement: Ronda 715li with date window
  • Strap: 2 x NATO issue straps (one black and one grey, 20mm)
  • Fixed Military Pattern Strap Bars

Benefits of Sapphire Crystal:

Many customers ask about the benefits of sapphire crystal over standard mineral crystal. Synthetic sapphire is the best material for watch crystals due to its strength and resistance to shattering and scratching. These characteristics make it highly desirable for military and security personnel, police officers, and outdoor enthusiasts, who make up over 70% of our customers. Watches with mineral glass crystals are more prone to damage, even when using hardened mineral crystals. The strength of sapphire crystals comes from a heat treatment process that removes internal stresses, followed by the application of two layers of anti-reflective coating. This durability is why sapphire crystal is also used in the latest iPhones. Although sapphire crystal comes at a higher price, it is typically only fitted to higher-end watches.

Experience the ultimate blend of durability, visibility, and long-term performance with the MWC G10SL/200, a timepiece built to withstand the most extreme conditions while providing unparalleled readability and functionality.

column2: MWC G10 200m/660ft Water resistant Stainless Steel Military Watch with Sapphire Crystal and Date - NATO Stock Number: NSN 6645-99-472-3228D
column3:
SKU Code: G10/100UKG/D

The MWC 200m/660ft water-resistant G10 models in stainless steel are among our longest-established G10/GS series watches, variants of which have been used by the military since the mid-1980s. This updated model has been assigned the NATO Stock Number NSN: 6645-99-472-3228 by the Ministry of Defence Codification Agency, confirming it as the most up-to-date approved specification (see the attached image of the MOD codification sheet).

This particular watch differs from others in the range due to a specific request from a military contractor, who asked for an upgraded version for one of his clients. While the original model submitted for NATO codification required only 100m water resistance, this version was enhanced to 200m, significantly exceeding the standard specification.

Key Features:

  • Scratch-Resistant Sapphire Crystal: Offers superior durability and clarity.
  • 10-Year Battery Life: Powered by the Swiss Ronda 715li movement, significantly reducing maintenance requirements.
  • Enhanced Water Resistance: The screw-down crown and screw caseback ensure the watch is water-resistant up to 200m/660ft. Non-Reflective Sandblasted Case: The light sandblasted finish, achieved by blasting the case with tiny glass or sand particles, gives it a matte appearance and minimizes light reflection.
  • Covert Design and Durability: Developed under contract specifications requiring a covert design, screw caseback, sapphire crystal, and screw-down crown, resulting in a low-maintenance and highly durable timepiece.

Additional Information:

  • Strap: Supplied with a current issue heat-welded grey NATO webbing strap in Admiralty Grey.
  • Case Back: Bears various contract markings in addition to the unique serial number.

Important Note: We frequently receive inquiries about which G10 model we supply for use by the British Army. For clarification, this is the model currently issued, the only difference being that UK procurement contracts for the MoD currently specify the non-date window variant.

Specifications:

  • Case Diameter:
    • Measuring from 2 o’clock to 8 o’clock: 38.5mm
    • Excluding the crown: 40mm
    • Including crown guard and crown: 43mm
  • Lug to Lug: 45.8mm
  • Thickness: 13.3mm
  • Weight: 66g
  • Fixed Solid Strap Bars
  • Dial Color: Black
  • Luminosity: Super Luminova C5
  • Case Material: 316L Stainless Steel
  • Caseback: Screw-down, 316L Stainless Steel
  • Crown: Screw-down, 316L Stainless Steel
  • Water Resistance: 20atm (200m/660ft)
  • Battery: CR2016 Lithium - Estimated life 10 years
  • Crystal: Sapphire with two layers of anti-reflective coating
  • Movement: Swiss Ronda 715li
  • Strap: Admiralty Grey 20mm Heat Welded NATO Strap

Benefits of Sapphire Crystal:

Sapphire crystal is the best material for watch crystals due to its exceptional strength and resistance to shattering and scratching. These qualities make it ideal for military and security personnel, police officers, and those with active outdoor lifestyles, who comprise over 70% of our customers.

When accidents involving watches result in cracked crystals, over 90% involve mineral glass crystals. Even with hardened mineral crystals, they do not match sapphire's durability in adverse conditions. Sapphire crystals undergo heat treatment to remove internal stresses, enhancing strength, and are then crafted into watch crystals with two layers of anti-reflective coating. Interestingly, sapphire crystal is now standard on the latest iPhones. While sapphire crystal is more costly, it is typically fitted only to higher-end watches due to its superior quality.

column2: MWC G10 200m/660ft Water resistant Black PVD Steel Military Watch with Sapphire Crystal and Date - NATO Stock Number: NSN 6645-99-493-1283D
column3:
SKU Code: G10/100UKGPV/D

The MWC G10 model in covert black PVD stainless steel, with a 200m/660ft water resistance rating, is one of our longest-established G10/GS series watches. Variants of this model have been supplied to military forces since the mid-1980s. This updated version has been allocated NATO Stock Number NSN: 6645-99-493-1283 by the UK Ministry of Defence Codification Agency, confirming it as the most current approved specification. (Please see the attached image for the MOD codification sheet.)

This particular watch differs from others in the range due to a specific request from a military contractor, who asked for an upgraded version for one of his clients. While the original model submitted for NATO codification required only 100m water resistance, this version was enhanced to 200m, significantly exceeding the standard specification.

This watch features a highly scratch-resistant sapphire crystal and an impressive 10-year battery life. Although this G10/GS specification watch traces its origins to our earliest G10 designs from the 1980s, it is thoroughly updated.

The screw-down crown and screw caseback significantly enhance water resistance, while the Swiss Ronda 715li movement with a 10-year battery life greatly reduces maintenance needs. The case has a light sandblasted, non-reflective finish, a process long used by military watch manufacturers to give the case a matte appearance that minimizes light reflection. This finish is achieved by blasting the watch case with tiny glass or sand particles, hence the term sandblasted.

The contract specifications for this model required it to be covert, with a screw caseback, 200m water resistance, sapphire crystal, and screw-down crown, resulting in a low-maintenance and highly durable timepiece. The watch is supplied with a current-issue heat-welded black NATO webbing strap.

The back of the case bears various contract markings, including the unique serial number and NATO NSN number.

Benefits of Sapphire Crystal:

Many people ask about the benefits of sapphire crystal over standard mineral crystal. Synthetic sapphire is the best material for watch crystals due to its strength and shatter and scratch resistance, making it highly appealing to military personnel, security personnel, police officers, and those with active outdoor lifestyles. These groups make up over 70% of our customers. When accidents occur, over 90% of watches with cracked crystals have mineral glass crystals. Even hardened mineral crystals do not match sapphire for durability under adverse conditions.

Sapphire crystals are heat-treated to remove internal stresses, enhancing their strength. Additionally, they receive two layers of anti-reflective coating. Sapphire crystal's robustness is why it's now used on the latest iPhones. However, due to its cost, sapphire crystal is typically found only on higher-end watches.

column2: MWC P656 2025 Model PVD Tactical Series Watch with GTLS Tritium, Sapphire Crystal and Ten Year Battery Life
column3:

The MWC Tactical Series P656 watches are built for durability and performance, featuring military-grade stainless steel cases and 300m water resistance. Built for Modern Demands

Inspired by mid-1990s designs, the P656 watches incorporate significant modern upgrades:

The G10LM is a contemporary General Service pattern military field watch, designed with specifications that distinguish it from many similar watches currently on the market.

Unlike many manufacturers who use a push-on caseback, the G10LM features a screw-down caseback, improving durability and enhancing water resistance. In addition, while many comparable watches use perspex or plexiglass crystals, MWC uses hardened mineral glass, which is more scratch resistant and gives the watch a cleaner and more refined appearance.

The G10LM range offers a wide variety of configurations including:

  • Stainless steel cases
  • Black PVD steel cases
  • European pattern 12-hour dials
  • U.S. NATO pattern 12/24-hour dials
  • Models with or without a date window
  • Various military strap colour options

One of the most notable differences between the G10LM and comparable watches from other manufacturers is the price. Similar models from other brands often retail for three times the price or more, making the G10LM an exceptional value.

column2: MWC NATO Pattern Stainless Steel Military Pilots Chronograph with Sapphire Crystal and Swiss Ronda Movement - 12 Hour GMT Bezel and GTLS/Tritium Illumination
column3:
SKU Code: TN5/BD/12

MWC NATO Chronograph – Stainless Steel (Ronda 5040.D Movement)

A Rugged Professional Chronograph with Dual Time Zone Tracking and, Tritium/GTLS Tubes and 200m Water Resistance

Built for the aviation applications and tough enough for any terrain, the MWC NATO chronograph in stainless steel was developed to meet the demanding needs of military aircrews, commercial pilots, and general aviation professionals. While based on the proven specifications used by other NATO chronograph manufacturers, this model brings several smart upgrades that improve functionality, legibility, and real-world practicality.

This model uniquely blends new and traditional technology, retaining all the popular features of our conventional G10 Luminova models while incorporating cutting-edge self-luminous GTLS tritium vials. The specification of this watch adheres to American Military Specification Number: US MIL-W-46374F.

Key Features:

Superior Night Visibility: The self-luminous system ensures exceptional readability at night. This model has a distinctive face layout, with the hour and minute hands each containing a brightly glowing vial of Tritium gas. The 12 o'clock position features an orange tube to assist with orientation if the watch is off the wrist, the other tubes are green. The vials contain less than the personal limit of 25 millicuries of radioactive gas, complying with U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission regulations (10 CFR 30.15).

Tritium GTLS Tubes: These military-grade watches are the most readable in low light or total darkness, thanks to the self-activated light source, which is up to 100 times brighter than traditional luminous paint. These light-emitting tubes require no battery power, charging, or maintenance, and have a life expectancy of at least 10 years.

Superior PVD Plating

The special covert matte black non-reflective PVD finish on this watch is often specified in military contracts for special forces, anti-terrorist units, and covert operations. In the PVD process, the steel itself isn’t black throughout; instead, the color is achieved through a specialized chemical plating technique. This method is far superior to the painted finishes found on lower-quality watches, which tend to fade or wear off easily. The PVD process alters the steel at a molecular level, ensuring long-lasting durability even under heavy use. However, the finish can be damaged if the case is gouged by metal or subjected to severe abrasion against rocks or similar materials.

At the heart of the watch is a high-performance Swiss Ronda 5040.D movement, delivering precise chronograph operation with zero-complication ease. The case measures 45.5mm including the crown, striking a balance between bold presence and wearable proportions.

Dual Time Zone Tracking – No Extra Hand Required

With more and more of our customers needing to track two time zones—whether for international operations, travel, or coordination across regions—we’ve opted for a simplified solution over a traditional GMT hand.

Instead of adding mechanical complexity, we’ve included a 12-hour bezel, allowing effortless time zone offset tracking. This bezel, unlike the more common dive-style “count-up” design, lets you read a second time zone at a glance:

Example:
If you're in Paris and need to track Miami time (6 hours behind), simply rotate the bezel so the 9 o’clock marker aligns with the 3 on the dial. Just like that, you’ve got two time zones on your wrist—no apps, no math, no fuss.

This feature is especially useful now that most divers rely on computers underwater, making the traditional dive bezel less essential. Here, it becomes a highly functional tool for everyday, multi-zone use—without compromising the watch's 20 ATM / 200m / 660ft water resistance or its classic diver-inspired style.

Ronda 5040.D Sub-Dial Functions

Left Sub-Dial (9 o'clock):

  • 30-minute counter: This counter is a subdial located at the 9 o'clock position on the watch face and records up to 30 minutes of elapsed time for the chronograph function.

Bottom Sub-Dial (6 o’clock):

Dual-Function 1/10-Second & 12-Hour Chronograph Counter

For the first 30 seconds after the chronograph is started, this dial functions as a 1/10-second counter, providing high-precision measurement. Once 30 minutes have elapsed, the same sub-dial switches to an hour counter, tracking up to 12 hours of total time.

Right Sub-Dial (3 o’clock):
Small Seconds Display
Displays continuous running seconds for standard timekeeping. This sub-dial runs independently of the chronograph function.

🕹️ Chronograph Controls:

Top pusher (Start/Stop): Starts and stops the chrono.

Bottom pusher (Reset): Resets the chronograph hands to zero (after it’s stopped).

📝 Pro Tip:

If the chrono hands are out of alignment (say, the 1/10th sec hand isn't zeroing properly), you can recalibrate them via the crown pulled out to the second position — each pusher moves a different hand. This means that whereas with many chronographs they need to be booked into a watchmaker to make minor adjustments. This does not apply in the case of this particular model because it is a simple job to find the calibration yourself if it's ever required.

Key Specifications

  • Movement: Swiss Ronda 5040.D
  • Battery: Renata 395
  • Battery Life: Approx. 54 months
  • Case Material: 316L Black PVD stainless steel
  • Case Diameter: 44mm (excluding crown), 45.5mm (including crown)
  • Lug-to-Lug: 49mm
  • Thickness: 13.5mm
  • Crown: Screw-down locking crown
  • Crystal: Sapphire with anti-reflective coating
  • Water Resistance: 20 ATM / 200m / 660ft
  • Dial: Matte black with Tritium GTLS Tubes for maximum Nighttime Visibility
  • Strap Bars: Shoulderless Spring Bars
  • Strap: Supplied with a black 20mm NATO nylon webbing strap

Why Sapphire?

The crystal is one of the most vulnerable parts of any watch—especially in demanding environments. That’s why we’ve equipped this model with a sapphire crystal, known for its exceptional scratch resistance and shatter strength.

In fact, over 90% of cracked crystal incidents we see involve mineral glass. Even hardened mineral simply doesn’t compare. Our sapphire crystals are strengthened through heat treatment to remove stress and double-coated with anti-reflective layers for clear visibility in bright light or at night.

This makes the watch ideal for military and security personnel, police, outdoor adventurers, and anyone who demands real-world reliability. Over 70% of our customers fall into one of these groups, and sapphire crystal is a must for their line of work.

The Bottom Line

The MWC NATO chronograph in stainless steel fuses military-grade durability with modern functionality—making it a versatile and robust timepiece ready for anything. Whether you're piloting, on duty, traveling between time zones, or simply want a watch that does more without overcomplicating things, this chronograph delivers—every time.

  • Dial: Matte black with Tritium GTLS Tubes for maximum Nighttime Visibility
  • Strap Bars: Shoulderless spring bars
  • Strap: Supplied with a black 20mm NATO nylon webbing strap

Why Sapphire?

The crystal is one of the most vulnerable parts of any watch—especially in demanding environments. That’s why we’ve equipped this model with a sapphire crystal, known for its exceptional scratch resistance and shatter strength.

In fact, over 90% of cracked crystal incidents we see involve mineral glass. Even hardened mineral simply doesn’t compare. Our sapphire crystals are strengthened through heat treatment to remove stress and double-coated with anti-reflective layers for clear visibility in bright light or at night.

This makes the watch ideal for military and security personnel, police, outdoor adventurers, and anyone who demands real-world reliability. Over 70% of our customers fall into one of these groups, and sapphire crystal is a must for their line of work.

The Bottom Line

The MWC NATO chronograph in stainless steel fuses military-grade durability with modern functionality—making it a versatile and robust timepiece ready for anything. Whether you're piloting, on duty, traveling between time zones, or simply want a watch that does more without overcomplicating things, this chronograph delivers—every time.

column2: MWC G10EL General Service Watch with Electroluminescent (EL) Backlit Dial
column3:
SKU: G10EL/BG/SS

The G10EL is a contemporary G10 / General Service pattern field watch, representing a very innovative take on other current G10 Models. 

Instead of relying solely on luminous paint, the G10EL features a thin electroluminescent panel integrated into the dial. When activated, it produces a uniform glow across the entire surface, ensuring the numerals and markers remain clearly visible in low light or total darkness.

For maximum usability, the watch also incorporates Luminova on the hands and dial. This combination of electroluminescence and luminous paint guarantees that the G10EL remains effortlessly readable in any lighting conditions.

A few key points:

  • Technology – EL panels work by passing an alternating current through phosphor layers, causing them to emit light. 
  • Operation – The G10EL dial has a button on the case that activates the backlight  briefly. This is important to maximise battery life.
  • Advantages – Even, full-dial illumination; readable in pitch black without waiting for lume to “charge” from a light source.

If you’ve ever pressed a button on a watch and seen the entire dial glow rather than just the hands and markers, that’s an EL dial in action.

The special covert matte black non-reflective PVD finish used for this watch is often specified in military contracts for many special forces units, anti-terrorist units, and various covert operations. The PVD process creates the color through a special chemical plating process, which is vastly superior to the painted finish used on some lower-quality watches that can easily fade or rub off. This PVD process alters the color of the steel at a molecular level, ensuring it does not deteriorate even during heavy usage, although it can be damaged if the case is gouged by a piece of metal or worn down by continuous heavy abrasion against rocks.

Built for practicality and reliability, the G10EL combines proven Seiko quartz movement with robust stainless steel construction and a water-resistant design.

column2: MWC GMT (Dual Time Zone) 200m/660ft Water resistant Military Watch in Black PVD Steel Case with Screw Crown
column3:
SKU Code: GMT/PVD/200M

Overview

The MWC GMT Dual Time Zone Watch is a rugged military-pattern GMT watch built for professionals and travelers alike. With a 316L stainless steel case in black PVD, a reliable Ronda 515.24 quartz movement, and 200m / 660ft water resistance, this watch is designed for extreme durability in any environment.

Special Covert PVD Finish

The covert matt black non-reflective PVD finish used for this watch is often specified in military contracts for special forces units, anti-terrorist units, and various covert operations. Unlike painted finishes on lower-quality watches that can fade or rub off, this PVD process alters the steel's color at a molecular level, ensuring durability even during heavy usage. However, the PVD finish can be damaged if the case is badly gouged or worn down by continuous heavy abrasion against hard surfaces.

Key Features

  • Dual Time Zone (GMT): Track two time zones with ease using the 24-hour hand.
  • High Performance Movement: Ronda 515.24 quartz with date window.
  • Professional Build: 316L stainless steel case with screw-down crown and caseback.
  • Superior Durability: 200m (20 ATM) water resistance, perfect for swimming, diving, and outdoor use.
  • Scratch-Resistant: Sapphire crystal with dual anti-reflective coating.
  • Military-Grade Design: Based on our 200m G10 general service watches, with contract markings and serial number.

Specifications

  • Case Diameter: 40mm (42mm including crown)
  • Lug to Lug: 46mm
  • Thickness: 13mm
  • Weight: 60g
  • Dial: Black with Super Luminova
  • Movement: Ronda 515.24 quartz
  • Case: 316L Black PVD Stainless Steel
  • Strap: 20mm NATO issue strap
  • Strap Bars: Drilled Lugs
  • Water Resistance: 20 ATM / 200m / 660ft
  • Glass: Sapphire crystal (shatter & scratch resistant)

Why Choose Sapphire Crystal?

Over 70% of our customers are military, police, or outdoor professionals who need gear they can rely on. From our experience, 90% of cracked watch crystals involve mineral glass, not sapphire.

  • Synthetic sapphire is heat-treated for maximum strength.
  • Provides exceptional scratch and shatter resistance.
  • Used in high-end timepieces and even iPhone screens for superior resilience.
  • Slightly higher cost, but ensures long-term reliability in tough conditions.

👉 Ideal for frequent travelers, pilots, military personnel, and anyone who needs to monitor multiple time zones with confidence.

column2: 10 x 18mm, 20mm, 22mm or 24mm Watch Pins Designed to Create the Appearance of a Military Watch with Fixed Solid Bars
column3:
Transform Your Watch with Military-Style Watch Pins

SKU: STB

Upgrade the look and durability of your watch with our Military-Style Watch Pins, designed to mimic the solid fixed bars found on authentic military timepieces. Whether you're modifying a civilian Seiko or Pulsar, or upgrading an existing MWC or CWC, these pins deliver a rugged, tactical appearance — without sacrificing functionality. Available in 18mm, 20mm, 22mm or 24mm

🔧 Key Features

✅ Authentic Military Look

  • Creates the appearance of solid fixed bars, transforming standard spring bar watches into military-style models.

  • Ideal for converting civilian watches into their tactical counterparts — as shown in our Seiko transformation images.

  • Compatible with major military watch brands like MWC, CWC, and Pulsar, all widely used by armed forces and law enforcement.

✅ Compatibility & Easy Installation

  • Drilled lugs? Simply push out the pins for removal.

  • No drilled lugs? Cut the pins in the middle for quick extraction.

  • Prefer simplicity? Quick-Release (QR) versions with button mechanisms are also available for hassle-free changes.

✅ Built for Durability

  • Stronger than regular spring bars, these are built to withstand the demands of military and tactical use.

  • Made from stainless steel to ensure long-term reliability and resistance to wear.

📸 Visual Transformation

  • Before & After Gallery: See the impact of the upgrade on Seiko and Diver models.

  • Comparison Close-Up: On our black PVD watch, one original spring pin is shown side-by-side with a military-style pin.

  • G10 Model Example: Showcases how convincingly a civilian watch can be transformed to match fixed-pin aesthetics.

  • Material: Stainless Steel

  • Sizes: 10 x 18mm, 20mm, 22mm or 24mm 

⭐ Proven Performance

Originally developed to solve a problem — converting 250 incorrectly assembled spring pin watches into fixed-bar lookalikes — these pins turned a logistical headache into a client-approved upgrade. The result? A clean, solid military appearance that’s both functional and visually striking.

Ready to upgrade?
These pins are ideal for collectors, enthusiasts, and anyone wanting a rugged, field-ready look without altering the watch’s core structure.

Originally developed to solve a problem — converting 250 incorrectly assembled spring pin watches into fixed-bar lookalikes — these pins turned a logistical headache into a client-approved upgrade. The result? A clean, solid military appearance that’s both functional and visually striking.

Ready to upgrade?
These pins are ideal for collectors, enthusiasts, and anyone wanting a rugged, field-ready look without altering the watch’s core structure.
